purpose: Defines the `!resume` command for CHAT SESSION resumption — NOT swarm resumption. The agent reviews the full conversation history backwards from most recent, identifies the original task / progress made / point of interruption / outlined plan, optionally checks files-being-modified state and git status to gather more context, presents a Status Summary, then picks up exactly where it left off. For SWARM/dashboard resumption use !track_resume or !p_track_resume instead.
gotchas: This is for CHAT session resumption only — NOT swarm/dashboard resumption (use !track_resume or !p_track_resume for those)
Read before assuming — always check actual file state and git status; don't trust conversation history claims about what was done (another agent or the user may have made changes)
Don't redo completed work — verify by checking file contents, not by relying on conversation
Preserve the original approach unless the previous approach clearly failed — don't switch strategies unnecessarily
Read as far back in history as needed — better to spend time understanding than to miss critical context
Use git status + git diff to identify in-progress uncommitted changes that may represent partial work
patterns: 4-step procedure: reconstruct from conversation history → gather additional context if insufficient → present Status Summary → resume execution
Status Summary format: Original task → Progress so far → Interrupted at → Remaining work
Resume strategy decision tree: mid-edit → complete the edit; mid-plan → next planned step; previous failed → analyze and try alternative; completed but unverified → run verification
conventions: Lighter command doc — no Output Files, no dashboard interaction, just behavior steps
Top callout explicitly redirecting swarm-resumption use cases elsewhere
Key Principles section at the bottom enumerates 4 guidelines (read before assuming, don't redo, preserve approach, be thorough)
